findings: MID feature (~30 min runtime). Narration 3776 words (~25.2 min @150wpm) sits inside the mid narration band 24.0-32.4 min (ADR-0008); the remaining runtime is designed music + breathing room (the surveillance-room silences and verdict beat). 53 spans; 20/20 claims used; no dangling refs; chapter/span sets match.

Voice gate (house standard): FK 6.06 (<=9.5); em-dashes 4.24/1000w (<=6); And/But/Because/So sentence-starts 11.0% (<=12%); rhetorical commands 2 ('Imagine', 'Sit') (<=3); banned-filler hits 0. Reads as spoken human cadence, not AI-written.

R2 discipline: Rajaratnam convicted at a jury trial stated as fact (verdict May 11, 2011, all 14 counts; sentence Oct 13, 2011, 11 years); conduct framed as 'the jury found / prosecutors proved / the government said'; the 'longest sentence' superlative attributed to prosecutors and time-bound to 2011; profit hedged and anchored to the ~$53.8M forfeiture.

Co-defendant separation enforced: Rajat Gupta presented as a SEPARATE trial/verdict/punishment (2 years), never merged with Rajaratnam's penalties; tippers (Goel/Intel, Kumar/McKinsey, Moffat/IBM, Chiesi/New Castle) stated as charged separately. SEC $92.8M civil penalty kept distinct from the criminal forfeiture+fine.

Pre-publish (R2 legal review): re-verify the verdict date/counts, the 11-year sentence, the $53,816,434 forfeiture + $10M fine, the $92,805,705 SEC penalty, the 2013 Second Circuit wiretap affirmance, and Gupta's separate disposition against primary DOJ/SEC/court records. Any verbatim wiretap-call dialogue must be confirmed against the public trial record before locking narration (CLM-0018). Verify memoir title/date before citing (CLM-0016). No real-person likeness (invariant 11).
